aiTitle: Fix traefik Host rule parameter error

content.thinking:
The user is getting a Traefik error about a routing rule with multiple Host parameters. The error says "unexpected number of parameters; got 2, expected one of [1]" which means the Host rule doesn't support multiple hosts in the comma-separated format anymore.

Let me look at the traefik configuration files to understand the issue.

---

  proxy:
    image: traefik:latest
    restart: always
    networks:

      - proxy_network
    ports:
      - 0.0.0.0:80:80
      - 0.0.0.0:443:443
      - 0.0.0.0:1080:1080
    environment:

      - DOCKER_API_VERSION=1.40
    volumes:
      - "/var/run/docker.sock:/var/run/docker.sock:ro"
      - "/var/log/traefik.log:/var/log/traefik.log:rw"
      - "./config/traefik/traefik.yml:/etc/traefik/traefik.yml:ro"
      - "./config/traefik/include.d/:/etc/traefik/dynamic/:ro"
      - "./config/traefik/certs:/certs:ro"
    labels:
      - "traefik.enable=true"
      - "traefik.http.routers.proxy.rule=Host(`traefik.${DOMAIN_USERNAME}.localhost`, `traefik.eliberty.loc`)"
      - "traefik.http.routers.proxy.service=api@internal"

content.thinking:
The issue is on line 24 of docker-compose-traefik.yml:

```
- "traefik.http.routers.proxy.rule=Host(`traefik.${DOMAIN_USERNAME}.localhost`, `traefik.eliberty.loc`)"
```

In newer versions of Traefik (v3), the `Host()` rule only accepts a single parameter. To match multiple hosts, you need to use `||` (OR) operator:

```
Host(`traefik.eliberty.localhost`) || Host(`traefik.eliberty.loc`)
```


This is a breaking change from v2, where the `Host()` matcher supported multiple comma-separated values in a single call.

content.text:
The issue is on [docker-compose-traefik.yml:24](docker-compose-traefik.yml#L24). Traefik v3 no longer accepts multiple parameters in `Host()` — you need to use `||` instead.

**Before:**
```
Host(`traefik.eliberty.localhost`, `traefik.eliberty.loc`)
```

**After:**
```
Host(`traefik.eliberty.localhost`) || Host(`traefik.eliberty.loc`)
```

7	    volumes:
8	      - ./config/mitmproxy/:/home/mitmproxy/.mitmproxy/
9	    networks:
10	      - proxy_network
11	    command: "mitmweb --web-host 0.0.0.0 --set ssl_insecure=true"
12	    labels:
13	      # (MITM WEB INTERFACE)
14	      - "traefik.enable=true"
15	      - "traefik.http.routers.mitmweb.rule=Host(`mitmweb.${DOMAIN_USERNAME}.localhost`, `mitmweb.eliberty.loc`)"
16	      - "traefik.http.routers.mitmweb.tls=true"
17	      - "traefik.http.routers.mitmweb.entrypoints=websecure"
18	      - "traefik.http.routers.mitmweb.service=mitmweb"
19	      - "traefik.http.routers.mitmweb.middlewares=host"
20	      - "traefik.http.services.mitmweb.loadbalancer.server.port=8081"
21	      # (MITM WEB INTERFACE) accepts only same host requests for websocket redirection, used for realtime refreshing
22	      - "traefik.http.middlewares.host.headers.customrequestheaders.Host=localhost:8081"
23	      - "traefik.http.middlewares.host.headers.customrequestheaders.Origin=http://localhost:8081"

file.content:
---
services:
  mitmproxy:
    image: mitmproxy/mitmproxy
    tty: true
    restart: on-failure
    volumes:
      - ./config/mitmproxy/:/home/mitmproxy/.mitmproxy/
    networks:
      - proxy_network
    command: "mitmweb --web-host 0.0.0.0 --set ssl_insecure=true"
    labels:
      # (MITM WEB INTERFACE)
      - "traefik.enable=true"
      - "traefik.http.routers.mitmweb.rule=Host(`mitmweb.${DOMAIN_USERNAME}.localhost`, `mitmweb.eliberty.loc`)"
      - "traefik.http.routers.mitmweb.tls=true"
      - "traefik.http.routers.mitmweb.entrypoints=websecure"
      - "traefik.http.routers.mitmweb.service=mitmweb"
      - "traefik.http.routers.mitmweb.middlewares=host"
      - "traefik.http.services.mitmweb.loadbalancer.server.port=8081"
      # (MITM WEB INTERFACE) accepts only same host requests for websocket redirection, used for realtime refreshing
      - "traefik.http.middlewares.host.headers.customrequestheaders.Host=localhost:8081"
      - "traefik.http.middlewares.host.headers.customrequestheaders.Origin=http://localhost:8081"

      # (MITM) for transparent use (force redirect your external endpoint to localhost with /etc/hosts and add a matching rule for traefik)
      - "traefik.http.routers.mitmproxyhttps.rule=Host(`docservicev3.rpstack.com`,`trinity.rpstage.com`, `trinity.rpstack.com`, `49.api.dp.rpstack.com`, `trinity.${DOMAIN_USERNAME}.loc`, `trinity.${DOMAIN_USERNAME}.localhost`)"
      - "traefik.http.routers.mitmproxyhttps.tls=true"
      - "traefik.http.routers.mitmproxyhttps.entrypoints=websecure"
      - "traefik.http.routers.mitmproxyhttps.service=mitmproxyhttps"
      - "traefik.http.services.mitmproxyhttps.loadbalancer.server.scheme=https"
      - "traefik.http.services.mitmproxyhttps.loadbalancer.server.port=8080"

      # (MITM) for transparent use - Redirect http to https
      - "traefik.http.routers.mitmproxyhttpredirect.rule=Host(`docservicev3.rpstack.com`)"
      - "traefik.http.routers.mitmproxyhttpredirect.entrypoints=web"
      - "traefik.http.routers.mitmproxyhttpredirect.middlewares=redirect-to-https@file"
      - "traefik.http.routers.mitmproxyhttpredirect.service=httpsredirector@file"

      # (MITM) to use like a real https proxy, curl -X https://localhost:1080 --proxy-cacert ./config/mitmproxy/mitmproxy-ca-cert.cer ...
      - "traefik.tcp.routers.mitmproxyhttps.rule=HostSNI(`*`)"
      - "traefik.tcp.routers.mitmproxyhttps.tls=true"
      - "traefik.tcp.routers.mitmproxyhttps.entrypoints=proxytcp"
      - "traefik.tcp.routers.mitmproxyhttps.tls.passthrough=true"
      - "traefik.tcp.routers.mitmproxyhttps.service=mitmproxyhttps"
      - "traefik.tcp.services.mitmproxyhttps.loadbalancer.server.port=8080"

      # (MITM) to use like a real http proxy, curl -X http://localhost ...
      - "traefik.tcp.routers.mitmproxy.rule=HostSNI(`*`)"
      - "traefik.tcp.routers.mitmproxy.entrypoints=proxytcp"
      - "traefik.tcp.routers.mitmproxy.service=mitmproxy"
      - "traefik.tcp.services.mitmproxy.loadbalancer.server.port=8080"

      - "Project=eliberty.mitm"

---

  mitmproxy:
    image: mitmproxy/mitmproxy
    tty: true
    restart: on-failure
    volumes:
      - ./config/mitmproxy/:/home/mitmproxy/.mitmproxy/
    networks:
      - proxy_network
    command: "mitmweb --web-host 0.0.0.0 --set ssl_insecure=true"
    labels:
      # (MITM WEB INTERFACE)
      - "traefik.enable=true"
      - "traefik.http.routers.mitmweb.rule=Host(`mitmweb.${DOMAIN_USERNAME}.localhost`, `mitmweb.eliberty.loc`)"
      - "traefik.http.routers.mitmweb.tls=true"
      - "traefik.http.routers.mitmweb.entrypoints=websecure"
      - "traefik.http.routers.mitmweb.service=mitmweb"
      - "traefik.http.routers.mitmweb.middlewares=host"
      - "traefik.http.services.mitmweb.loadbalancer.server.port=8081"
      # (MITM WEB INTERFACE) accepts only same host requests for websocket redirection, used for realtime refreshing
      - "traefik.http.middlewares.host.headers.customrequestheaders.Host=localhost:8081"
      - "traefik.http.middlewares.host.headers.customrequestheaders.Origin=http://localhost:8081"

      # (MITM) for transparent use (force redirect your external endpoint to localhost with /etc/hosts and add a matching rule for traefik)
      - "traefik.http.routers.mitmproxyhttps.rule=Host(`docservicev3.rpstack.com`,`trinity.rpstage.com`, `trinity.rpstack.com`, `49.api.dp.rpstack.com`, `trinity.${DOMAIN_USERNAME}.loc`, `trinity.${DOMAIN_USERNAME}.localhost`)"
      - "traefik.http.routers.mitmproxyhttps.tls=true"
      - "traefik.http.routers.mitmproxyhttps.entrypoints=websecure"
      - "traefik.http.routers.mitmproxyhttps.service=mitmproxyhttps"
      - "traefik.http.services.mitmproxyhttps.loadbalancer.server.scheme=https"
      - "traefik.http.services.mitmproxyhttps.loadbalancer.server.port=8080"

      # (MITM) for transparent use - Redirect http to https
      - "traefik.http.routers.mitmproxyhttpredirect.rule=Host(`docservicev3.rpstack.com`)"
      - "traefik.http.routers.mitmproxyhttpredirect.entrypoints=web"
      - "traefik.http.routers.mitmproxyhttpredirect.middlewares=redirect-to-https@file"
      - "traefik.http.routers.mitmproxyhttpredirect.service=httpsredirector@file"

      # (MITM) to use like a real https proxy, curl -X https://localhost:1080 --proxy-cacert ./config/mitmproxy/mitmproxy-ca-cert.cer ...

      - "traefik.tcp.routers.mitmproxy.entrypoints=proxytcp"
      - "traefik.tcp.routers.mitmproxy.service=mitmproxy"
      - "traefik.tcp.services.mitmproxy.loadbalancer.server.port=8080"

      - "Project=eliberty.mitm"

      - "traefik.http.routers.mitmweb.tls=true"
      - "traefik.http.routers.mitmweb.entrypoints=websecure"

      # (MITM WEB INTERFACE) accepts only same host requests for websocket redirection, used for realtime refreshing
      - "traefik.http.middlewares.host.headers.customrequestheaders.Host=localhost:8081"
      - "traefik.http.middlewares.host.headers.customrequestheaders.Origin=http://localhost:8081"

      # (MITM) for transparent use (force redirect your external endpoint to localhost with /etc/hosts and add a matching rule for traefik)
      - "traefik.http.routers.mitmproxyhttps.rule=Host(`docservicev3.rpstack.com`,`trinity.rpstage.com`, `trinity.rpstack.com`, `49.api.dp.rpstack.com`, `trinity.${DOMAIN_USERNAME}.loc`, `trinity.${DOMAIN_USERNAME}.localhost`)"
      - "traefik.http.routers.mitmproxyhttps.tls=true"
      - "traefik.http.routers.mitmproxyhttps.entrypoints=websecure"
      - "traefik.http.routers.mitmproxyhttps.service=mitmproxyhttps"
      - "traefik.http.services.mitmproxyhttps.loadbalancer.server.scheme=https"
      - "traefik.http.services.mitmproxyhttps.loadbalancer.server.port=8080"

      # (MITM) for transparent use - Redirect http to https
      - "traefik.http.routers.mitmproxyhttpredirect.rule=Host(`docservicev3.rpstack.com`)"
      - "traefik.http.routers.mitmproxyhttpredirect.entrypoints=web"
      - "traefik.http.routers.mitmproxyhttpredirect.middlewares=redirect-to-https@file"
      - "traefik.http.routers.mitmproxyhttpredirect.service=httpsredirector@file"

      # (MITM) to use like a real https proxy, curl -X https://localhost:1080 --proxy-cacert ./config/mitmproxy/mitmproxy-ca-cert.cer ...
      - "traefik.tcp.routers.mitmproxyhttps.rule=HostSNI(`*`)"
      - "traefik.tcp.routers.mitmproxyhttps.tls=true"
      - "traefik.tcp.routers.mitmproxyhttps.entrypoints=proxytcp"
      - "traefik.tcp.routers.mitmproxyhttps.tls.passthrough=true"
      - "traefik.tcp.routers.mitmproxyhttps.service=mitmproxyhttps"
      - "traefik.tcp.services.mitmproxyhttps.loadbalancer.server.port=8080"

      # (MITM) to use like a real http proxy, curl -X http://localhost ...
      - "traefik.tcp.routers.mitmproxy.rule=HostSNI(`*`)"
      - "traefik.tcp.routers.mitmproxy.entrypoints=proxytcp"
      - "traefik.tcp.routers.mitmproxy.service=mitmproxy"
      - "traefik.tcp.services.mitmproxy.loadbalancer.server.port=8080"

      - "Project=eliberty.mitm"
